title: play kube: Allow the user to import the contents of a tar file into a volume
body:
Add a new annotation to allow the user to point to a local tar file If the annotation is present, import the file's content into the volume Add the annotation to the documentation
Add an E2E test to the new annotation

Signed-off-by: Ygal Blum <ygal.blum@gmail.com>

```release-note
Play Kube - support volume import using a new annotation
```

comments.body:

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Changes LGTM, but tests are not happy. There is a gofmt error, please run `gofmt -s` on your files and push again.

> How would this work for `podman-remote kube play`?
> 
> Since it relates to a local path, I would prefer supporting it only for the local Linux-native Podman and error out for remote.
> 
> In order to do that, we need to pass the information down whether the request came from a remote client (see https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/api/handlers/libpod/kube.go#L103).
> 
> @rhatdan @baude @umohnani8 PTAL

@vrothberg Not sure I agree. Why block someone from importing a file on the remote machine?
@mheon @TomSweeneyRedHat @umohnani8, sorry for the delay in my response I was OOO. Please take a look

> @vrothberg Not sure I agree. Why block someone from importing a file on the remote machine?

We don't support certain podman-specific extensions already (e.g., the build feature).  We could certainly do it but it would complicate the REST API substantially.

Reading local paths on the server side (controlled by the client) is not acceptable for security reasons.  So all client-side data has to be send over to the server which in turn requires client-side preprocessing.

I am not necessarily against doing that but it is a lot of work, and requires a thorough design and design doc that we won't iron out in this PR.

> > @vrothberg Not sure I agree. Why block someone from importing a file on the remote machine?
> 
> We don't support certain podman-specific extensions already (e.g., the build feature). We could certainly do it but it would complicate the REST API substantially.
> 
> Reading local paths on the server side (controlled by the client) is not acceptable for security reasons. So all client-side data has to be send over to the server which in turn requires client-side preprocessing.
> 
> I am not necessarily against doing that but it is a lot of work, and requires a thorough design and design doc that we won't iron out in this PR.

Following offline discussion, added code to block import on remote requests
